sections.paragraph:
δυσμαθής, ές ‘pert. to being hard to learn’ (δυς-±μαθής; in this sense Aeschyl. et al.), then act. pert. to lacking sharpness or quickness in understanding, obtuse, slow to learn, hard to teach (so Pla. et al.; Cebes 35, 2; Philo, Mos. 2, 261) of complacent Christians Hs 9, 22, 1.—DELG s.v. μανθάνω.

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

act. act. = active

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Cebes Cebes , prominent in Platonic dialogues; ref. is to a dialogue associated with his name, I a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 265
